headline: Saudi Arabia shuts in 15 million barrels per day amid Hormuz crisis
body: Saudi Arabia has shut in approximately 15M b/d of crude oil production as the Strait of Hormuz crisis deepens, according to industry sources. The shut-in represents over half of the kingdom's total production capacity. Global oil markets reacted sharply with Brent crude surging past $120 per barrel. Analysts warn that prolonged shut-ins could lead to permanent reservoir damage.
